name: Vegetables in Spicy Cream Sauce
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces green beans, trimmed slices diagonally
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast cumin and coriander in a small skillet over medium-low heat until aromatic, stirring occasionally for about 4 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
2. Heat vegetable oil in the same skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook until golden brown, stirring occasionally for about 8 minutes.
3. Transfer onion mixture to a blender. Add cumin and coriander, half and half, cashews, and catsup. Blend until smooth and thick like a cream sauce.
4. Melt butter in a heavy medium skillet over medium heat. Add cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves; cook until aromatic for about 2 minutes.
5. Add tomato to the pan and cook for 1 minute. Then add all remaining vegetables (cauliflower, peas, carrot, green beans) and stir-fry for 3 minutes.
6. Stir in water, raisins, salt, cayenne pepper, cream sauce, and remaining half and half. Bring mixture to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until vegetables are tender, about 12 minutes.
7. Discard the cinnamon stick. (This dish can be prepared up to 2 days ahead; let it cool, cover, and refrigerate. Reheat before serving.)
8. Garnish with cilantro and serve.
